subject: Geography
title: The Indus Valley
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Indus Valley.

hook.text: Around 2500 BCE, the Indus River fed a civilization larger than ancient Egypt.

buildup.text: Cities like Mohenjo-daro had grid streets, sewage systems, and public baths.

discovery.text: The Indus people traded with Mesopotamia but left a script no one can read.

twist.text: Around 1900 BCE, the civilization vanished. The river may have shifted course.

climax.text: A changing river likely turned fertile land to desert, scattering the population.

punchline.text: One river shift erased a civilization.

quiz.question: What happened to the Indus Valley civilization around 1900 BCE?
options: It vanished, possibly due to river changes
